goal: Build a benchmark harness for the speaker classifier + AEC and replace the unverified accuracy claims with measured numbers. scripts/conduit_tui/benchmark.py generates a small synthetic-voice corpus (formant-synth user vs formant-synth bot), enrols the classifier, measures (a) clean-speech accuracy, (b) accuracy with bot-as-interferer at -10dB, (c) Equal Error Rate over all clips, (d) AEC ERLE in pure-echo scenario, (e) correlation of cleaned mic vs user-only under double-talk. Also accepts real wav files via --user / --bot for environment-specific measurement. First run revealed: classifier 80% on synthetic (literature claim was 85-90%), AEC ERLE -3.2 dB on pure-sinusoid pathological input (vs 11.5 dB on broadband speech in earlier ad-hoc test). README updated with honest numbers + the synthetic-vs-real disclaimer.
